title: pilosella tubulata subsp. cymelliflora
intro: Pilosella tubulata subsp. cymelliflora is a subspecies of the Pilosella genus, commonly known as the purple-flowered mouse-ear hawk's-beard, which is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Pilosella tubulata subsp. cymelliflora include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and act as an antibacterial agent. Pilosella tubulata subsp. cymelliflora is typically prepared as a decoction or tincture, and it can also be found in herbal supplements, teas, and infusions.
